content_en:
him: ‘All the camels, cows, sheep, and slaves that you see are your wages’. So he said: ‘O servant of Allah, do not mock me’. So I said: ‘1am not mocking you’. So he took all of that and led them away and did not leave any of it. O Allah if] did that seeking Your Face, then release us from our situation’. So the rock moved and they walked out of the cave

This hadith is clear proof that one of the means of tawassul is mentioning one’s good deeds while making dua. And this fact is, in and of itself, a great incentive for one to do deeds purely for the sake of Allah, so that one may use them as a means of tawassul when one is in dire need of a du’ a being answered.

5. Tawassul Through Mentioning the Effect of the dua

Tawassul can also be made by attaching the response of the du‘ a to a good deed or benefit. So, for example, a person can say: “O Allah! I ask you to increase me in knowledge, so that I may teach

my family and relatives your religion.” Or, he might pray: “O Allah! Increase my wealth so that I can spend it in your way!”

This principle is shown by the hadith in which the Prophet (#8),

When a person comes to visit the sick, then let him say: O

Allah! Cure your servant so-and-so, for he will then inflict a 364

wound on an enemy, or walk for your sake to the prayer.

It is also shown in the du’ a of Ibrahim when he left Hajar and — Isma'il in the barren valley of Bakkah, later to be called Makkah:

a icabakietbodate De es LIT EE 4 CH KE aids, SANS Bh ites?

363 Reported by al-Bukhari (3/260/), Muslim (# 6607), al-Nasa’i and others, with various wordings and additions.

364 Reported by Aba Dawid (# 3107). See Sahih al-Jami', # 466.

O My Lord! I have left my family to live in a valley that has no fruits, close to your Sacred House, so that they may establish the prayer. Our Lord! Therefore cause a group of people to love them, and provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks.”

So Ibrahim asked that Allah bless them with fruits, and cause a community of people to live with them, so that they could establish prayer and give thanks to Allah.

This is also demonstrated in Miisa’s dua : CN SIS OS LATO sg haS sh

O My Lord! Give me a helper from my family. My brother Haran. Increase my strength through him. And let him share my task. That we may glorify you a lot. And remember you much. Verily, you are ever a Well-Seer over us.

Musa asked Allah to bless him with Haran as a helper so that both of them could thank Him and praise Him.

6. Tawassul by Asking a Living Person to Make Du‘ a

It is allowed to make tawassul by asking a living person to make dua on your behalf, if you feel that such a person is a true and righteous person. So, it is permissible to go to a scholar, and say, for example: “O so-and-so! I ask that you make a du‘ @ to Allah that he cure my son from such-and-such an illness”.

Safwan ibn ‘Abdillah narrated: “I went to Syria, and visited Aba al-Darda in his home, but he was not present when [ arrived. Umm al-Darda asked me, ‘Are you going to perform Hajj this year?’ I replied that I was, so she said: ‘In that case, do not forget to pray to

36 seerah Ibrahim, 37.

Allah for us for good, for the Prophet (3%) used to say:

“The dua@ of a Muslim for his brother in his absence is responded to. In his presence there is an angel that has been assigned to him; every time he makes a du‘@ for his brother with good, the ange! assigned to him says, ‘Amin. And to you

In this narration, Umm al-Darda asked Safwan to remember her and her husband while making dua.

Jabir ibn ‘Abdillah reported that a woman came to the Prophet (#8) and said: “Pray for me and my husband”. So the Prophet (#8)

Once, during the caliphate of ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab, there was a severe drought that afflicted Madinah. The Muslims, under the leadership of ‘Umar, went outside the city to perform salah al-istisqa, the prayer for rain. After finishing the prayer, ‘Umar said: “O Allah! We used to perform tawassul through the Prophet (#%), (while he was alive), and now we will perform tawassu/ through the Prophet’s (#8) uncle, ‘Abbas ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib”. So saying, he commanded ‘Abbas to make a dw’ @ to Allah for rain, and indeed, Allah responded to his du’ a, and it rained in Madinah.”

Although it is permissible to ask others to make dua for one’s self, it is better not to do so for personal du’ as (this is in contrast to du as that are related to the benefit of the entire wmmah, such as the - preceding example, in which ‘Umar asked Abbas to make a dua for the Muslims of Madinah).

36? Reported by Muslim, Ibn Majah, and Ahmad, from Abi al-Darda, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jami' # 3380.

38Reported by Abi: Dawad (1530). The Arabic wording is ‘Salla Allahu “alayki...’, which shows the permissibility of using this phrase for other than the Prophet (38). This because, when used for the Prophet (3%), this is a du‘a to Allah to honour and bless him, but when used for someone else, it is a general prayer to have mercy on him.

36 Reported by al-Bukhari (4/209) and others.

This for the following reasons:

The general rule is that the person should pray for himself. Just like he does his other acts of worship, and takes care of his own affairs of this world, so too should he make his own dua.

Asking others is a type of request that is done to other than Allah, and shows a need or poverty that a person feels towards mankind. In this there might be a type of humiliation for a person, and a degradation of his status as he beseeches others.

There is no one that will feel more sincerity than the one making du ‘a for himself and his loved ones. It cannot be expected that a friend or distant acquaintance will feel the same sincerity and emotions that the person himself is feeling. And yet it is these very emotions that are the cause of any du’ a being accepted. So how is it that a person will go to a total stranger, and ask him to pray to Allah about a problem that he himself is facing?

This act leads many people to leave dua themselves, as they then rely on others to make du’ @ for them. This fact was mentioned earlier.

It might lead to arrogance on the part of the person being asked. For it is possible that he will feel that he is holy and pious, and hence why people come to him to ask him to make dua for them.

It has not been reported that any of the major Companions ever asked the Prophet (#8) to make du’ a for them regarding a personal, worldly benefit that they desired. They would ask him to make dua regarding a matter that was of benefit to all of the Muslims (such as ‘Umar’s request to the Prophet (2%), to make dua to bless the Muslims with food when the army ran short of it during the Battle of Tabak), or ofa religious ben-

efit (such as Aba Hurayrah asking the Prophet (3) to make dua to guide his mother to Islam), but there is no reported incident in which they came to the Prophet (3%) to ask for a personal, worldly benefit.”

Ithas been reported that once a person came to Malik ibn Deenar, one of the famous scholars of his time. He asked Malik: “Make dua for me, as I am in severe circumstances”. Malik responded, “In that case, make dua for yourself, as He is the One Who responds to the distressed who is in severe circumstances”. In another incident, Tawis, one of the scholars of the a7 ‘in, visited a sick person, who asked him: “O Tawis, please make a du'‘a for me”. Tawis replied: “Pray for yourself, for He is the One that responds to the one in distress”.

These narrations show thatit is a mistake to ask someone to make du'@ on your behalf, and then rely on that person’s du‘ a, without making du’ @ yourself.

So these are the six ways that are found in the Quran and Sunnah by which tawassul can be done.

3721 should be mentioned that some scholars only mention three ways of . performing tawassul (Shaykh al-Albani), others mention four (Shaykh Bakr), and yet others mention seven (Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymin). In reality, there is no contradiciton in these various categorisations, and if one ponders over them, one will see that many of these categories are merely sub-categories of other types. For example, tawassul by mentioning Allah’s favours can be considered a type of tawassul through Allah’s Names and Attributes (since the favour is an effect of Allah’s Names), and tawassul by mentioning the good effect of one’s dua can be considered zawassu/ through a good deed (since it is the intention to do a good deed in the future, which, in and of itself, is a good deed). However, these six categories that are mentioned here seem, and Allah knows best, to be the simplest way of expressing the various types of zawassul.

7. Other Types of Tawassul

The types of tawassul given above are the only types sanctioned by the Quran and Sunnah. All other types of tawassul are not sanctioned by Islam. This because all acts of worship, including zawassul, must be based on authentic texts of the Qur’an and Sunnah, and not upon opinion or desire.

So if a person does tawassul by a means other than what has been mentioned, then without a doubt he will either fall into shirk (associating partners with Allah) or did’ ah (innovation). For example, if a person makes zawassul to another object or false deity, this is one

. of the greatest types of shirk! To make tawassul through idols and other false deities is exactly what the pagan Arabs did before the Prophet’s (#%) advent.
content_id:
kepadanya: 'Semua unta, sapi, kambing, dan budak yang engkau lihat adalah upahmu'. Maka dia berkata: 'Wahai hamba Allah, jangan mengejekku'. Maka aku berkata: 'Aku tidak mengejekmu'. Maka dia mengambil semua itu dan membawanya pergi tanpa meninggalkan sedikit pun. Ya Allah, jika aku melakukan itu demi mencari Wajah-Mu, maka bebaskanlah kami dari keadaan ini'. Maka batu itu bergeser dan mereka keluar dari gua tersebut.

Hadith ini merupakan bukti jelas bahwa salah satu cara tawassul adalah menyebutkan amal-amal saleh seseorang ketika berdoa. Dan kenyataan ini, dengan sendirinya, merupakan motivasi besar bagi seseorang untuk melakukan amal secara ikhlas karena Allah, sehingga ia dapat menggunakannya sebagai sarana tawassul ketika ia sangat membutuhkan du'a yang dikabulkan.

5. Tawassul Melalui Penyebutan Dampak dari Du'a

Tawassul juga dapat dilakukan dengan mengaitkan pengabulan du'a dengan amal saleh atau manfaat tertentu. Misalnya, seseorang dapat berkata: "Ya Allah! Aku memohon kepada-Mu untuk menambah ilmuku, agar aku dapat mengajarkan

kepada keluarga dan kerabatku agama-Mu." Atau, ia dapat berdoa: "Ya Allah! Tambahkanlah hartaku agar aku dapat membelanjakannya di jalan-Mu!"

Prinsip ini ditunjukkan oleh hadith di mana Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

Apabila seseorang menjenguk orang sakit, hendaklah ia berkata: Ya

Allah! Sembuhkanlah hamba-Mu si fulan, karena ia akan memerangi 364

musuh demi-Mu, atau berjalan demi-Mu menuju salah.

Hal ini juga ditunjukkan dalam du'a Ibrahim ketika beliau meninggalkan Hajar dan Isma'il di lembah tandus Bakkah, yang kemudian disebut Makkah:

a icabakietbodate De es LIT EE 4 CH KE aids, SANS Bh ites?

363 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (3/260/), Muslim (no. 6607), al-Nasa'i dan lainnya, dengan berbagai redaksi dan tambahan.

364 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (no. 3107). Lihat Sahih al-Jami', no. 466.

Ya Tuhanku! Aku telah menempatkan keluargaku untuk tinggal di lembah yang tidak memiliki buah-buahan, dekat dengan Rumah-Mu yang Suci, agar mereka mendirikan salah. Ya Tuhan kami! Maka jadikanlah hati sebagian manusia cenderung kepada mereka, dan berilah mereka rezeki berupa buah-buahan agar mereka bersyukur."

Maka Ibrahim memohon agar Allah memberkahi mereka dengan buah-buahan, dan menjadikan sekelompok manusia tinggal bersama mereka, agar mereka dapat mendirikan salah dan bersyukur kepada Allah.

Hal ini juga ditunjukkan dalam du'a Musa: CN SIS OS LATO sg haS sh

Ya Tuhanku! Berilah aku seorang penolong dari keluargaku. Saudaraku Harun. Tambahkanlah kekuatanku melalui dia. Dan biarkanlah dia berbagi tugasku. Agar kami banyak memuliakan-Mu. Dan banyak mengingat-Mu. Sesungguhnya Engkau Maha Melihat atas kami.

Musa memohon kepada Allah untuk memberkahi beliau dengan Harun sebagai penolong agar keduanya dapat bersyukur dan memuji-Nya.

6. Tawassul dengan Meminta Orang yang Masih Hidup untuk Berdoa

Diperbolehkan melakukan tawassul dengan meminta orang yang masih hidup untuk mendoakan anda, jika anda merasa bahwa orang tersebut adalah orang yang benar dan saleh. Maka diperbolehkan mendatangi seorang ulama dan berkata, misalnya: "Wahai fulan! Aku memintamu untuk mendoakan kepada Allah agar Dia menyembuhkan putraku dari penyakit ini dan itu".

Safwan ibn 'Abdillah meriwayatkan: "Aku pergi ke Syam, dan mengunjungi Abu al-Darda di rumahnya, tetapi beliau tidak ada ketika aku tiba. Umm al-Darda bertanya kepadaku, 'Apakah engkau akan menunaikan haji tahun ini?' Aku menjawab ya, maka dia berkata: 'Kalau begitu, jangan lupa berdoa kepada

36 Surah Ibrahim, 37.

Allah untuk kami dengan kebaikan, karena Nabi (ﷺ) pernah bersabda:

"Du'a seorang Muslim untuk saudaranya di saat ketidakhadirannya akan dikabulkan. Di hadapannya ada malaikat yang ditugaskan untuknya; setiap kali ia mendoakan saudaranya dengan kebaikan, malaikat yang ditugaskan kepadanya berkata, 'Amin. Dan untukmu juga

Dalam riwayat ini, Umm al-Darda meminta Safwan untuk mendoakan dirinya dan suaminya ketika berdoa.

Jabir ibn 'Abdillah meriwayatkan bahwa seorang wanita datang kepada Nabi (ﷺ) dan berkata: "Doakanlah aku dan suamiku". Maka Nabi (ﷺ)

Suatu ketika, pada masa kekhalifahan 'Umar ibn al-Khattab, terjadi kekeringan parah yang melanda Madinah. Kaum Muslimin, di bawah kepemimpinan 'Umar, keluar dari kota untuk melaksanakan salah al-istisqa, salah meminta hujan. Setelah selesai salah, 'Umar berkata: "Ya Allah! Kami dahulu bertawassul melalui Nabi (ﷺ), (ketika beliau masih hidup), dan sekarang kami akan bertawassul melalui paman Nabi (ﷺ), 'Abbas ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib". Seraya berkata demikian, beliau memerintahkan 'Abbas untuk berdoa kepada Allah meminta hujan, dan memang Allah mengabulkan doanya, dan hujan pun turun di Madinah."

Meskipun diperbolehkan meminta orang lain untuk mendoakan diri kita, lebih baik tidak melakukannya untuk du'a-du'a pribadi (ini berbeda dengan du'a yang berkaitan dengan kemaslahatan seluruh ummah, seperti contoh sebelumnya, di mana 'Umar meminta Abbas untuk mendoakan kaum Muslimin Madinah).

367 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, Ibn Majah, dan Ahmad, dari Abu al-Darda, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' no. 3380.

368 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (1530). Redaksi Arabnya adalah 'Salla Allahu 'alayki...', yang menunjukkan kebolehan menggunakan ungkapan ini untuk selain Nabi (ﷺ). Hal ini karena, ketika digunakan untuk Nabi (ﷺ), ini adalah du'a kepada Allah untuk memuliakan dan memberkahi beliau, tetapi ketika digunakan untuk orang lain, ini adalah doa umum untuk merahmati orang tersebut.

369 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (4/209) dan lainnya.

Hal ini karena alasan-alasan berikut:

Prinsip umumnya adalah seseorang hendaknya berdoa untuk dirinya sendiri. Sebagaimana ia melakukan ibadah-ibadahnya sendiri dan mengurus urusan duniawinya sendiri, demikian pula hendaknya ia berdoa sendiri.

Meminta kepada orang lain adalah jenis permintaan yang ditujukan kepada selain Allah, dan menunjukkan kebutuhan atau kefakiran yang dirasakan seseorang terhadap manusia. Dalam hal ini mungkin terdapat sejenis kehinaan bagi seseorang, dan penurunan martabatnya ketika ia memohon-mohon kepada orang lain.

Tidak ada seorang pun yang akan merasakan keikhlasan lebih besar daripada orang yang berdoa untuk dirinya sendiri dan orang-orang yang dicintainya. Tidak dapat diharapkan bahwa seorang teman atau kenalan jauh akan merasakan keikhlasan dan emosi yang sama seperti yang dirasakan orang itu sendiri. Dan justru emosi-emosi inilah yang menjadi sebab diterimanya du'a. Maka bagaimana mungkin seseorang mendatangi orang asing dan memintanya berdoa kepada Allah mengenai masalah yang ia sendiri hadapi?

Perbuatan ini menyebabkan banyak orang meninggalkan du'a untuk diri mereka sendiri, karena mereka kemudian bergantung pada orang lain untuk mendoakan mereka. Fakta ini telah disebutkan sebelumnya.

Hal itu mungkin menimbulkan kesombongan pada pihak orang yang dimintai. Karena mungkin ia akan merasa bahwa dirinya suci dan saleh, dan oleh karena itulah orang-orang datang kepadanya untuk meminta didoakan.

Tidak pernah diriwayatkan bahwa ada seorang pun dari sahabat utama yang pernah meminta Nabi (ﷺ) untuk mendoakan mereka mengenai keuntungan duniawi pribadi yang mereka inginkan. Mereka meminta beliau mendoakan mengenai hal yang bermanfaat bagi seluruh kaum Muslimin (seperti permintaan 'Umar kepada Nabi (ﷺ) untuk mendoakan agar Allah memberkahi kaum Muslimin dengan makanan ketika pasukan kehabisan bekal dalam Perang Tabuk), atau manfaat keagamaan

(seperti Abu Hurayrah yang meminta Nabi (ﷺ) mendoakan agar ibunya mendapat hidayah masuk Islam), tetapi tidak ada riwayat di mana mereka datang kepada Nabi (ﷺ) untuk meminta keuntungan duniawi pribadi."

Diriwayatkan bahwa suatu kali seseorang datang kepada Malik ibn Deenar, salah seorang ulama terkenal pada masanya. Orang itu meminta Malik: "Doakanlah aku, karena aku dalam keadaan sangat sulit". Malik menjawab: "Kalau begitu, berdoalah untuk dirimu sendiri, karena Dialah yang mengabulkan doa orang yang dalam kesulitan". Dalam kejadian lain, Tawus, salah seorang ulama tabi'in, menjenguk orang sakit yang memintanya: "Wahai Tawus, tolong doakan aku". Tawus menjawab: "Berdoalah untuk dirimu sendiri, karena Dialah yang mengabulkan doa orang yang dalam kesulitan".

Riwayat-riwayat ini menunjukkan bahwa adalah suatu kesalahan meminta seseorang mendoakan anda, lalu bergantung pada doa orang tersebut, tanpa berdoa sendiri.

Demikianlah enam cara yang terdapat dalam al-Quran dan Sunnah untuk melakukan tawassul.

372 Perlu disebutkan bahwa sebagian ulama hanya menyebutkan tiga cara tawassul (Syaikh al-Albani), yang lain menyebutkan empat (Syaikh Bakr), dan yang lainnya lagi menyebutkan tujuh (Syaikh Ibn al-'Utsaimin). Pada hakikatnya, tidak ada pertentangan dalam berbagai pengkategorian ini, dan jika seseorang merenungkannya, ia akan melihat bahwa banyak dari kategori-kategori ini sekadar merupakan sub-kategori dari jenis lainnya. Misalnya, tawassul dengan menyebutkan nikmat-nikmat Allah dapat dianggap sebagai jenis tawassul melalui Nama-nama dan Sifat-sifat Allah (karena nikmat adalah dampak dari Nama-nama Allah), dan tawassul dengan menyebutkan dampak baik dari du'a seseorang dapat dianggap sebagai tawassul melalui amal saleh (karena itu adalah niat untuk melakukan amal saleh di masa depan, yang dengan sendirinya merupakan amal saleh). Namun demikian, enam kategori yang disebutkan di sini tampaknya, dan Allah yang Maha Mengetahui, merupakan cara paling sederhana untuk mengungkapkan berbagai jenis tawassul.

7. Jenis-Jenis Tawassul Lainnya

Jenis-jenis tawassul yang disebutkan di atas adalah satu-satunya jenis yang disahkan oleh Quran dan Sunnah. Semua jenis tawassul lainnya tidak disahkan oleh Islam. Hal ini karena semua ibadah, termasuk tawassul, harus didasarkan pada dalil-dalil yang sahih dari Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah, dan bukan berdasarkan pendapat atau keinginan semata.

Maka jika seseorang melakukan tawassul dengan cara selain dari apa yang telah disebutkan, tanpa keraguan ia akan jatuh ke dalam shirk (menyekutukan Allah) atau bid'ah (perkara baru dalam agama). Misalnya, jika seseorang bertawassul kepada benda lain atau sesembahan palsu, maka ini adalah salah satu

bentuk shirk yang paling besar! Bertawassul melalui berhala-berhala dan sesembahan palsu lainnya adalah persis seperti yang dilakukan oleh bangsa Arab jahiliah sebelum diutusnya Nabi (ﷺ).

O الله إذا] did that seeking Your Face, then release us from our situation’. لذا the rock moved and they walked out of the cave This الحديث is clear proof that one of the means of tawassul is mentioning one’s الخير deeds بينما making الدعاء. And this fact is, in and of itself, a great incentive for one to do deeds purely for the sake of الله, لذا that one may use them as a means of tawassul عندما one is in dire need of a du’ a being answered. 5. Tawassul Through Mentioning the Effect of the الدعاء Tawassul can أيضاً be made by attaching the response of the du‘ a to a الخير deed or benefit. لذا, على سبيل المثال, a person can say: “O الله! I ask you to increase me in العلم, لذا that I may teach my الأسرة and relatives your religion.” Or, he might pray: “O الله! It is أيضاً shown in the du’ a of إبراهيم ﷺ عندما he left Hajar and — Isma'il in the barren valley of Bakkah, later to be called مكة المكرمة: a icabakietbodate De es LIT EE 4 CH KE aids, SANS Bh ites? 363 Reported by البخاري (3/260/), مسلم (# 6607), al-Nasa’i and others, with various wordings and additions. 364 Reported by Aba Dawid (# 3107). See صحيح al-Jami', # 466. O My Lord! I have left my الأسرة to live in a valley that has no fruits, close to your Sacred House, لذا that they may establish the الصلاة. Our Lord! لذلك cause a group of people to المحبة them, and provide them with fruits لذا that they may give thanks.” لذا إبراهيم ﷺ asked that الله bless them with fruits, and cause a الجماعة of people to live with them, لذا that they could establish الصلاة and give thanks to الله. This is أيضاً demonstrated in Miisa’s الدعاء : CN SIS OS LATO sg haS sh O My Lord! Give me a helper from my الأسرة. My الأخ Haran. Increase my القوة through him. And let him share my task. That we may glorify you a lot. And remember you much. Verily, you are ever a Well-Seer over us. موسى ﷺ asked الله to bless him with Haran as a helper لذا that both of them could thank Him and praise Him. 6. Tawassul by Asking a Living Person to Make Du‘ a It is allowed to make tawassul by asking a living person to make الدعاء on your behalf, إذا you feel that such a person is a true and الصالح person. لذا, يجوز to go to a scholar, and say, على سبيل المثال: “O لذا-and-لذا! I ask that you make a du‘ @ to الله that he الدواء my son from such-and-such an المرض”. Safwan ibn ‘Abdillah narrated: “I went to Syria, and visited Aba al-Darda in his home, but he was not present عندما [ arrived. Umm al-Darda asked me, ‘Are you going to perform الحج this year?’ I replied that I was, لذا sقال: ‘In that case, do not forget to pray to 36 seerah إبراهيم ﷺ, 37. الله for us for الخير, for النبي ﷺ (3%) used to say: “The الدعاء@ of a مسلم for his الأخ in his absence is responded to. In his presence there is an angel that has been assigned to him; every time he makes a du‘@ for his الأخ with الخير, the ange! assigned to him says, ‘آمين. And to you In this narration, Umm al-Darda asked Safwan to remember her and her الزوج بينما making الدعاء. Jabir ibn ‘Abdillah reported that a woman came to النبي ﷺ (#8) and said: “Pray for me and my الزوج”. لذا النبي ﷺ (#8) حين, خلال the الخلافة of ‘عمر ibn al-Khattab, there was a severe drought that afflicted المدينة المنورة. The المسلمون, under the leadership of ‘عمر, went outside the city to perform الصلاة al-istisqa, the الصلاة for rain. بعد finishing the الصلاة, ‘عمر said: “O الله! We used to perform tawassul through النبي ﷺ (#%), (بينما he was alive), and now we will perform tawassu/ through النبي ﷺ’s (#8) uncle, ‘Abbas ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib”. لذا saying, he commanded ‘Abbas to make a dw’ @ to الله for rain, and indeed, الله responded to his du’ a, and it rained in المدينة المنورة.” وإن كان يجوز to ask others to make الدعاء for one’s self, it is better not to do لذا for personal du’ as (this is in contrast to du as that are related to the benefit of the entire wmmah, مثل the - preceding example, in which ‘عمر asked Abbas to make a الدعاء for the المسلمون of المدينة المنورة). 36? Reported by مسلم, ابن ماجه, and أحمد, from Abi al-Darda, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jami' # 3380. 38Reported by Abi: Dawad (1530). The Arabic wording is ‘Salla Allahu “alayki...’, which shows the permissibility of using this phrase for other than النبي ﷺ (38). This لأن, عندما used for النبي ﷺ (3%), this is a du‘a to الله to honour and bless him, but عندما used for someone else, it is a general الصلاة to have الرحمة on him. 36 Reported by البخاري (4/209) and others. This for the following reasons: The general rule is that the person should pray for himself. Just like he does his other acts of worship, and takes care of his own affairs of this الدنيا, لذا أيضاً should he make his own الدعاء. Asking others is a type of request that is done to other than الله, and shows a need or الفقر that a person feels towards mankind. In this there might be a type of humiliation for a person, and a degradation of his status as he beseeches others. There is no one that will feel more sincerity than the one making du ‘a for himself and his loved ones. It cannot be expected that a friend or distant acquaintance will feel the same sincerity and emotions that the person himself is feeling. And yet it is these very emotions that are the cause of any du’ a being accepted. لذا how is it that a person will go to a total stranger, and ask him to pray to الله about a problem that he himself is facing? This act leads many people to leave الدعاء themselves, as they then rely on others to make du’ @ for them. This fact was mentioned earlier. It might lead to الغرور on the part of the person being asked. For it is possible that he will feel that he is holy and pious, and ومن ثَم why people come to him to ask him to make الدعاء for them. It has not been reported that any of the major الصحابة ever asked النبي ﷺ (#8) to make du’ a for them فيما يخص a personal, worldly benefit that they desired. They would ask him to make الدعاء فيما يخص a matter that was of benefit to all of the المسلمون (مثل ‘عمر’s request to النبي ﷺ (2%), to make الدعاء to bless the المسلمون with food عندما the army ran short of it خلال the Battle of Tabak), or ofa religious ben- efit (مثل Aba Hurayrah asking النبي ﷺ (3) to make الدعاء to guide his الأم to الإسلام), but there is no reported incident in which they came to النبي ﷺ (3%) to ask for a personal, worldly benefit.” Ithas been reported that حين a person came to مالك ibn Deenar, one of the famous العلماء of his time. He asked مالك: “Make الدعاء for me, as I am in severe circumstances”. مالك responded, “In that case, make الدعاء for yourself, as He is the One Who responds to the distressed who is in severe circumstances”. In another incident, Tawis, one of the العلماء of the a7 ‘in, visited a sick person, who asked him: “O Tawis, please make a du'‘a for me”. Tawis replied: “Pray for yourself, for He is the One that responds to the one in distress”. These narrations show thatit is a mistake to ask someone to make du'@ on your behalf, and then rely on that person’s du‘ a, without making du’ @ yourself. لذا these are the six ways that are found in the القرآن الكريم and السنة by which tawassul can be done. 3721 should be mentioned that some العلماء only mention three ways of . performing tawassul (Shaykh al-Albani), others mention four (Shaykh Bakr), and yet others mention seven (Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymin). In reality, there is no contradiciton in these various categorisations, and إذا one ponders over them, one will see that many of these categories are merely sub-categories of other types. على سبيل المثال, tawassul by mentioning الله’s favours can be considered a type of tawassul through الله’s Names and Attributes (بما أن the النعمة is an effect of الله’s Names), and tawassul by mentioning the الخير effect of one’s الدعاء can be considered zawassu/ through a الخير deed (بما أن it is the intention to do a الخير deed in the future, which, in and of itself, is a الخير deed). غير أن, these six categories that are mentioned here seem, and الله knows best, to be the simplest الطريق of expressing the various types of zawassul. 7. Other Types of Tawassul The types of tawassul given above are the only types sanctioned by the القرآن الكريم and السنة. All other types of tawassul are not sanctioned by الإسلام. This لأن all acts of worship, بما في ذلك zawassul, must be based on authentic texts of the Qur’an and السنة, and not upon opinion or desire. لذا إذا a person does tawassul by a means other than what has been mentioned, then without a doubt he will either fall into الشرك (associating partners with الله) or did’ ah (البدعة). على سبيل المثال, إذا a person makes zawassul to another object or false deity, this is one . of the greatest types of الشرك! To make tawassul through idols and other false deities is exactly what the pagan Arabs did قبل النبي ﷺ’s (#%) advent.
